Alright, let's check out your podcast flow charts. You've got two of them. The first one is your podcast idea mind map that's going to help you get the perfect idea for your podcast. And next you've got the podcast life cycle, which really just shows the whole flow of a podcast from the beginning to the end. So let's jump in to the mind map first. So if you notice here on the left hand side, this is for show ideas. Basically, what do you want your general podcast to be all about? It gives you a lot of topics to work from some different formats. And keep in mind that you can actually mix up your show. It doesn't have to be the same every single time, but this should give you a lot of ideas to work from. And then when you go to actually do an individual, an episode, this will give you the thought joggers just to kind of think like, Hey, what is it that people have been asking for where can I go out and find ideas? And then also how can I find guests for my podcast? So this will give you all the great places to look. So you have a never ending stream of awesome guests. And next we'll look at the life cycle. So really, you know, this process might vary a little bit, but as long as you kind of stick to this, generally speaking, you'll be fine. Now keep in mind, we've provided a lot more details in the checklist as well as in the manual. So generally speaking, you're going to record your show. It's either going to be recorded as a video or as an audio. You're going to edit the file. You're either going to do that yourself or you're going to outsource that to a company. We've provided recommendations of where to do it. And then you're going to create any sort of show notes that you need, maybe an image. And you might want to do these things yourself. But again, you can outsource these if you want to. And then after you've done all this preparation, then you're going to go and you're going to upload your podcast to your hosting. And we have recommended Libsyn for your hosting, but there's several others that you can use. Just keep in mind that once you upload your podcast, you only have to do that once. And what it's going to do is going to add it to your RSS feed and then automatically be syndicated to all of the various places that you've submitted your podcast. Now you are going to need to go and actually create accounts with these various podcast networks. Keep in mind, one thing to realize is that iTunes or Apple Podcast does take a little bit longer than others. And then there are some of the various podcast directories that are dependent upon Apple. So this might be where you want to start. But once you get going, you'll, you know, you want to be on Stitcher, you want to be on Spotify and everywhere else. And as long as you have set up all of your directories correctly and they're all hooked to your RSS feed, all of this distribution will automatically happen for you. So after you've added it to your hosting, then you're going to want it to add it to your blog, probably as like a blog page on your website or whatever it might be. And then once you've got it added to your website, then you promote it anywhere that you possibly can. So if you've got social media, email anywhere that you can shout it from a top of a mountain, just to get the word out, and you're going to want to send people, you know, to probably the bigger podcast networks as well as to your webpage, whatever is best for you. But as long as you kind of understand this is the flow of getting the thing made, you get it on hosting, you get it on your website, and then you promote these things. If you understand the life cycle, you'll get better every single time and it will get easier. Go make it happen.
